title: Lifecycle, Storage Classes, Archive Restore, and Cost · Language Lens: Archive Restore State
summary: A policy transitions active content immediately, expires current versions but not hidden history, and promises instant archive access without retrieval or minimum-duration cost. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the archive restore state increment in lifecycle and storage-cost decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Request, monitor, authorize, and communicate temporary archive restoration with tier, expiry, duplicate request, and failure states.
Model filters, current and noncurrent transitions, expiration, delete markers, multipart cleanup, precedence, propagation, and unsupported bucket features.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Archive Restore State · Theory Archive Restore State
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3142 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to evaluate lifecycle transition, retention lock, restore, and minimum-duration cost; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
